page: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is a Nitro-based instance featuring 96 virtual CPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes hyperthreading with 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per physical core. This instance does not include local storage or standard GPUs, but it features a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is categorized as weak in stress-ng and average in Geekbench. However, multi-core CPU performance is strong in stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks. Cryptographic operations and extended instructions also perform in the top tier, while database operations and Redis SET operations demonstrate strong throughput.

The primary tradeoff of the dl2q.24xlarge lies in its single-threaded processing limits, as shown by the lower single-core benchmarks. However, its high resource density, featuring 768.0 GB of RAM and 96 vCPUs, offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that can leverage parallel processing. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by the 100 Gbps baseline network speed.
This instance is suited for memory-intensive parallel workloads, large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is an x86_64 Nitro-based instance configured with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores using Intel Xeon 8259CL processors at 2.5 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. While it lacks local storage and standard GPUs, it provides a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, whereas multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations are strong. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-dense, highly parallelized workloads. It is best suited for large-scale database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is a Nitro instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for parallel, memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network speed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense, high-bandwidth virtual server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and seven-hundred sixty-eight gigabytes of RAM for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Strong multi-core CPU and Redis SET performance
Suited for large databases, caching layers, and parallel processing
